summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Inventory Search-First page refactor. All 13 backend tests passed (100%). All 19 frontend UI tests passed (100%). The new inventory page implements search-first behavior with cyan/teal theme, right-side filter drawer, filter tags, minimal column table, and bulk selection/update functionality.

backend: Inventory GET without search - returns paginated data
Inventory search with query 'pa' - returns matching results
Inventory filter by status 'healthy' - filters correctly
Inventory filter options endpoint - returns categories/statuses
Bulk Update location field - updates products
Bulk Update discount_percent field - updates products
Bulk Update with invalid field - returns 400 error
Bulk Update with missing SKUs - returns 400 error
Inventory requires auth - returns 401 without token
Bulk Update requires auth - returns 401 without token
Products requires auth - returns 401 without token
frontend: Empty state 'Ready to manage stock?' on page load (search-first behavior)
View Low Stock button with data-testid
Summary cards (Total Items, Low Stock, Expiring Soon)
Search triggers results with 2+ chars (debouncing works)
Table columns correct (Medicine, Total Stock, Location, Discount %, Nearest Expiry, Status, Actions)
Bulk Update button appears when items selected
Bulk Update modal opens with field select
Filter drawer has all filters (Category, Dosage Type, Schedule Type, GST %, Location, Stock Status)
Filter drawer has Apply and Reset buttons
Filter tag appears after applying filter and can be removed

critical_code_review_comments: InventorySearch.js implements search-first pattern correctly - shows empty state until user searches or applies filters
Debouncing implemented with 500ms delay and minimum 2 characters
Filter drawer opens from right side with all required filters
Filter tags are removable individually with X button and 'Reset All' option
Bulk selection works with checkboxes and shows selection count
Bulk Update modal has field selection dropdown and value input
Backend /api/products/bulk-update endpoint validates allowed fields and handles errors properly
All interactive elements have proper data-testid attributes for testing
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_inventory_search.py - Created new test file for inventory search feature
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing products in database.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Inventory Search-First page fully tested and working. Route is /inventory which loads InventorySearch.js. Key features: search-first design (no auto-load), 500ms debounce with 2+ char requirement, right-side filter drawer with Category/Dosage/Schedule/GST/Location/StockStatus filters, filter tags that can be removed individually, minimal column table, bulk selection with checkboxes, bulk update modal, Add Stock modal, Excel Bulk Upload wizard. Backend bulk update API at /api/products/bulk-update supports fields: location, discount_percent, gst_percent, category, schedule, brand. Test credentials: testadmin@pharmacy.com/admin123
